Ukraine Conflict News





23 December 2025 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • US boat strike kills another person in eastern Pacific as Trump escalates pressure on Venezuela Press TV 23 Dec 2025 -- A US military strike on a vessel in the eastern Pacific Ocean has killed another individual, marking the latest fatal attack in Washington's expanding campaign of lethal force at sea.
  • Russia denies reports of embassy evacuation from Venezuela IRNA 23 Dec 2025 -- Russia's Foreign Ministry has strongly denied media reports claiming that Moscow has begun evacuating its embassy in Venezuela, calling the allegations false and misleading.
  • Venezuela demands end to US military presence Press TV 23 Dec 2025 -- Venezuela has called for an "immediate cessation" of US military deployments in the Caribbean.

  • Statement From Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell on National Guard Support to Louisiana DoD 23 Dec 2025 -- Yesterday, the Secretary of War authorized the activation of up to 350 Louisiana National Guard members on Title 32 status through February 28, 2026.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • US unveils plan for 'Trump-class' new battleship, sparking feasibility doubts in modern naval warfare Global Times 23 Dec 2025 -- The US on Monday announced its plan to build a new class of battleships that is allegedly even larger than their World War II-era predecessors - but equipped with missiles. A Chinese expert noted that the US' new battleships are of a new genre of warship, but remained skeptical about their feasibility in modern naval warfare given their huge size.
  • Trump Says US Intends to Commission Up to 25 Units of Newly Unveiled Battleships Sputnik 23 Dec 2025 -- US President Donald Trump said on Monday that his government intends to commissions the construction of up to 25 units of the newly announced "Trump class" battleship.

  • Department of War Invests $32.7M to Accelerate Solid Rocket Motor Component Production DoD 23 Dec 2025 -- The Department of War announced today two September 30, 2025, Defense Production Act (DPA) Title III investments totaling $32.7 million to expand the solid rocket motor (SRM) industrial base.
  • US Soldiers strengthen Tunisian Armed Forces' civil-military operations capacity Army News 23 Dec 2025 -- U.S. Soldiers provided Tunisian Armed Forces and Brigade des Forces Spéciales with training in civil affairs operations at Camp Ramadia, Tunisia, Oct. 6-24, strengthening civil-military capacity.
  • 2025 marks historic year for Guard members Army News 23 Dec 2025 -- For many Guard members, 2025 was a busy year.
  • Illinois Guard's 725th Transportation Company kept European Command fueled Army News 23 Dec 2025 -- About 160 Soldiers of the Illinois Army National Guard's Machesney Park-based 725th Transportation Company celebrated a deployment to U.S. European Command in which the unit completed more than 93 real-world missions, supported two major multinational exercises and delivered more than 2.1 million gallons of fuel, all while making personal and professional improvements in themselves.
  • U.S. Coast Guard to return Station Pascagoula to full operations US Coast Guard 23 Dec 2025 -- The U.S. Coast Guard announced Tuesday it will return Station Pascagoula in Pascagoula, Mississippi to full response operations.

  • MoD presents an updated classification system for mine action technologies Ukraine MOD 23 Dec 2025
  • Government streamlines relocation and rapid restoration of Ukraine's defense industry production facilities Ukraine MOD 23 Dec 2025
  • MoD launches a comprehensive overhaul of the rehabilitation system for defenders Ukraine MOD 23 Dec 2025

  • Chinese FM responds to Pentagon's report claiming China may have already deployed over 100 ICBMs in its three newest missile silo fields Global Times 23 Dec 2025 -- When asked to comment on a draft report from the US Pentagon claims that China may have already deployed over 100 intercontinental ballistic missiles in its three newest missile silo fields and shows no willingness to engage in arms control negotiations, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian stated that he is not aware of the so-called internal report from the US.
  • Pentagon's report claiming China's deployment of ICBMs a consistent US tactic to find excuses for its own nuclear strength modernization: FM Global Times 23 Dec 2025 -- When asked to comment on a draft report from the US Pentagon claims that China may have already deployed over 100 intercontinental ballistic missiles in its three newest missile silo fields and shows no willingness to engage in arms control negotiations, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian stated that he is not aware of the so-called internal report from the US.
  • Pentagon's claim of China's ICBM a pretext for US to upgrade nuclear power: FM Global Times 23 Dec 2025 -- A draft Pentagon report claimed China has likely loaded more than 100 ICBMs in silo fields, Reuters reported on Monday. Chinese military observers noted that the Pentagon's reports are full of speculation and aim to hype up the so-called China threat rhetoric.
  • Pentagon report on ballistic missiles cliché of hyping up 'China threat' rhetoric: experts Global Times 23 Dec 2025 -- A draft Pentagon report claimed China has likely loaded more than 100 ICBMs in silo fields, Reuters reported on Monday. Chinese military observers noted that the Pentagon's annual reports are full of speculation and aim to hype up the so-called China threat rhetoric.

  • CISC underscores strategic importance of critical minerals for defence preparedness and Viksit Bharat 2047 India PIB 23 Dec 2025 -- The Centre for Joint Warfare Studies (CENJOWS), in collaboration with IP Bazzaar, hosted Tech Talk, a high-level, closed-door roundtable discussion on the theme "Minerals That Matter: Geopolitics, Sovereignty & Value Chains" on 17 December 2025 in New Delhi

  • Why Israel Now Eyes Iran's Missiles Over Nukes Ahead Of Netanyahu-Trump Talks RFE/RL 23 Dec 2025 -- As Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu prepares to meet US President Donald Trump in Florida on December 29, the agenda centers on Iran - with a twist.
  • Iranian missile pierced window of Israeli intel chief's office in June, says senior general Press TV 23 Dec 2025 -- The spokesman for the Iranian Armed Forces has revealed details regarding the precision of Iranian missile technology, saying that a Fattah hypersonic missile successfully entered through the window of a senior Israeli military intelligence official's office during the 12-day war of June.

  • Tokyo's plan to boost coast guard including increasing patrol vessels amid military expansion moves particularly dangerous, further exposes militaristic ambition, expert warns Global Times 23 Dec 2025 -- Against the backdrop of recent series of worrying military expansion moves, the Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi administration is openly stating its maritime ambitions with the latest intent to advance the enhancement of the Japan Coast Guard's capabilities, including by increasing the number of large patrol vessels, according to TV Asahi on Tuesday. The report cited the so-called China's maritime advancement as excuse.

  • Libya's army chief killed in Ankara plane crash after high-level talks with Turkish officials Press TV 23 Dec 2025 -- Libya's chief of general staff was killed when a military aircraft crashed shortly after departing Ankara following an official visit to Turkey, Libyan and Turkish officials say.

  • Commander of the Royal Bahrain Naval Force, Rear Admiral Ahmed Mohamed Ebrahim Al Bin Ali, visited Naval Headquarters, Islamabad and called on Admiral Naveed Ashraf, NI, NI (M), Chief of the Naval Staff. ISPR Pakistan 23 Dec 2025
  • Field Marshal Syed Asim Munir, NI (M), HJ, COAS & CDF, visited the National Defence University (NDU), Islamabad today, where he was briefed by panel of civil and military participants of ongoing National Security and War Course on their academic perspective about challenges to national security and imperatives for overcoming them. ISPR Pakistan 23 Dec 2025

  • Lithuania purchases antitank ammunition and disposable grenade launchers Lithuania MND 23 Dec 2025 -- Lithuania is strengthening its defensive capability with a new purchase; the Defence Materiel Agency has signed a contract with Sweden's manufacturer Saab Dynamics on acquisition of antitank equipment.

  • PH Navy deploys landing craft BRP Manobo to Visayas Philippine News Agency 23 Dec 2025 -- The Philippine Navy (PN) has beefed up its forces in the Visayas with the deployment of its newly-repaired landing craft BRP Manobo (LC-297) to Naval Forces Central (NFC).

  • Announcement of Actions to Combat the Global Censorship-Industrial Complex US Dept. of State 23 Dec 2025 -- The State Department is taking decisive action against five individuals who have led organized efforts to coerce American platforms to censor, demonetize, and suppress American viewpoints they oppose.

  • Trump renews call to take over Greenland People's Daily 23 Dec 2025 -- U.S. President Donald Trump on Monday reiterated his call to take over Greenland, which triggered strong objections from Denmark and the European Union.

  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Call with Palau President Whipps US Dept. of State 23 Dec 2025
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with French Foreign Minister Barrot US Dept. of State 23 Dec 2025

Defense Industry

  • Contracts For Dec. 23, 2025

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • 'We will never leave all of Gaza': Israel's war minister signals permanent occupation Press TV 23 Dec 2025 -- Israel has no intention of fully withdrawing from Gaza and is planning new military and agricultural outposts in the territory's north, Israeli war minister Israel Katz declared in an open rejection of the ceasefire terms agreed with Hamas.

  • Israeli forces kill two Palestinians in Gaza City amid repeated ceasefire violations Press TV 23 Dec 2025 -- Two more Palestinians were shot dead by Israeli forces in Gaza on Monday, marking the latest fatalities amid persistent violations of a ceasefire agreement.

  • 1.6 million Palestinians face acute food insecurity in Gaza: UNRWA Press TV 23 Dec 2025 -- The United Nations agency for Palestinian refugees (UNRWA) has warned that 1.6 million people in Gaza face acute food insecurity, highlighting the territory's deteriorating humanitarian crisis amid gross violations of the ceasefire agreement by Israel.

  • Assault on 14 Children in the West Bank and the Martyrdom of 67 Others in Gaza since the Ceasefire ----- Occupation Forces Demolish 17 Homes and Displace 100 Palestinians in Al-Quds Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 23 Dec 2025 -- The cumulative tally of Palestinian martyrs from 7 October 2023 to 22 December 2025 has reached 72,044 martyrs, in addition to 180,486 injured across various Palestinian territories

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, Syria, Ukraine UNOCHA 23 Dec 2025

  • Security Council Press Statement on Yemen UN Security Council 23 Dec 2025

  • Terror attack kills five police officers in northwestern Pakistan IRNA 23 Dec 2025 -- An attack on police officers guarding oil facilities in northwestern Pakistan has killed five personnel.

  • Myanmar: Türk warns against violence and intimidation ahead of military-controlled vote OHCHR 23 Dec 2025 -- Myanmar is witnessing intensified violence, repression and intimidation ahead of military-controlled elections on 28 December, with civilians being threatened by both the military authorities and armed groups opposing the military regarding their participation in the polls, the UN Human Rights Office said on Tuesday.
  • Myanmar elections marked by fear as UN warns civilians are coerced from all sides UN News 23 Dec 2025 -- As Myanmar approaches elections scheduled for 28 December, the UN's top human rights official has said that civilians are being coerced from all sides - forced by the military to vote and threatened by armed opposition groups to boycott - in a climate of fear, violence and mass repression.

  • Thai FM expresses appreciation for China's role in deescalation of Thailand-Cambodia border conflict: media Global Times 23 Dec 2025 -- Thai Foreign Ministry spokesperson Nikorndej Balankura said on Tuesday that Thailand appreciates China's role in helping ease tensions and de-escalate the situation in the Thai-Cambodian border conflict, Xinhua News Agency reported on Tuesday.
  • Chinese special envoy conducts shuttle diplomacy again on Cambodia-Thailand border clashes Global Times 23 Dec 2025 -- From December 18 to 23, Deng Xijun, the Chinese Foreign Ministry's Special Envoy for Asian Affairs visited Cambodia and Thailand and engaged in shuttle diplomacy over the recent border clashes between the two countries, the Chinese Foreign Ministry said in a release on Tuesday.

  • 'Stand on right side of history': Sudan's PM urges Security Council to endorse initiative to end war Press TV 23 Dec 2025 -- Sudan's Prime Minister Kamil Idris has appealed to the UN Security Council to endorse his peace initiative aimed at halting the African country's devastating war and placing it under international oversight.
  • Briefing to the Security Council by Ms. Edem Wosornu, Director of Crisis Response Division, OCHA, on behalf of Mr. Tom Fletcher, USG for Humanitarian Affairs and Emergency Relief Coordinator, on the humanitarian situation in Sudan UNOCHA 23 Dec 2025

  • DR Congo: Displaced people in South Kivu close to 'utter desperation', WFP says UN News 23 Dec 2025 -- An estimated 500,000 people have been forced from their homes since fighting erupted in South Kivu province, Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), at the beginning of December.
  • WFP scaling up assistance to people displaced by violence in eastern DRC as resources risk running out WFP 23 Dec 2025 -- The United Nations World Food Programme (WFP) is scaling up to deliver life-saving assistance to more than 210,000 of the most vulnerable people displaced by the recent violence in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC). While some food supplies are already pre-positioned in the conflict area, WFP urgently needs USD 67M million to continue assistance for three months.

  • Security Council Re-Authorizes African Union Support, Stabilization Mission in Somalia, Adopting Resolution 2809 (2025) UN Security Council 23 Dec 2025 -- The Security Council today extended the authorization of the African Union Support and Stabilisation Mission in Somalia (AUSSOM) until 31 December 2026.
  • This resolution authorises AUSSOM to continue its essential role in strengthening stability and security in Somalia: UK Explanation of Vote at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 23 Dec 2025 -- Explanation of Vote by Ambassador Archie Young, UK Deputy Permanent Representative to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Somalia.

News Reports

  • Press conference - Parliament House, Canberra Australia DOD 23 Dec 2025
  • DFA Statement Philippines DFA 23 Dec 2025 -- The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) has taken note of recent local and foreign media reports referring to the Philippines' connection with the Bondi Beach incident in Sydney.

  • Meeting of Russia's Maritime Board President of Russia 23 Dec 2025 -- The Maritime Board of the Russian Federation held its meeting.
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's remarks at the General Meeting of the Commission of the Russian Federation for UNESCO, December 23, 2025 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 23 Dec 2025

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Lin Jian's Regular Press Conference on December 23, 2025 PRC MofA 23 Dec 2025
  • China opposes US' decision to add all foreign drones to 'Covered List' under pretext of 'national security', vows measures to protect Chinese firms: MOFCOM Global Times 23 Dec 2025 -- China firmly opposes the US decision to include all foreign-produced drone systems and their critical components on the "Covered List" under the pretext of "national security," a spokesperson for the Ministry of Commerce said on Tuesday.
  • Hong Kong SAR: UN experts dismayed by Jimmy Lai conviction OHCHR 23 Dec 2025 -- UN experts* today expressed deep disappointment at the conviction of pro-democracy media entrepreneur, Jimmy Lai, despite prolonged engagement with authorities on the case, and called for his immediate release from prison on humanitarian grounds.
  • China's reusable Long March-12A rocket completes maiden flight, enters orbit for basic success: report Global Times 23 Dec 2025 -- China launched Long March-12A reusable rocket at the Dongfeng Commercial Aerospace Innovation Test Zone at 10 am on Tuesday. The second stage of the rocket entered the preset orbit, but the first stage recovery failed. The cause is under further analysis and investigation, according to the China National Space Administration (CNSA).

  • KMT, TPP to begin impeachment proceedings against President Lai Friday CNA 23 Dec 2025 -- Taiwan's opposition parties have said they will open impeachment proceedings against President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) Friday over his failure to implement recent legal amendments, despite practical obstacles that make their passage a near impossibility.
  • Referendums rejecting Constitutional Court rulings 'unprecedented': DPP CNA 23 Dec 2025 -- The Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) legislative caucus said on Tuesday that there is "no precedent" in democratic countries for using referendums to overturn or reject Constitutional Court rulings.
  • Taipei proposes talks with S. Africa in January over rep office: MOFA CNA 23 Dec 2025 -- Taiwan's Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MOFA) has proposed talks with the South African government in January to resolve a year-long controversy over Taiwan's de facto embassy in South Africa, a diplomat said Tuesday.
  • Taiwan trying to counter China's 'long-arm jurisdiction': MOFA CNA 23 Dec 2025 -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MOFA) is "taking action" to prevent Taiwan nationals from facing possible arrest when visiting the China-friendly countries Laos, Cambodia and Belarus, it said Tuesday.
  • 5 undersea cables planned to boost communications resilience: Minister CNA 23 Dec 2025 -- Taiwan will add two international and three domestic undersea communications cables as part of its efforts to strengthen communications resilience, Minister of Digital Affairs Lin Yi-jing (林宜敬) said Tuesday.
  • Economic ties no longer boost Taiwan-China relations: Expert CNA 23 Dec 2025 -- Economic ties are no longer boosting cross-Taiwan Strait relations as Taiwanese businesses continue to shift investment away from China amid deepening U.S.-Beijing decoupling, an expert said at a seminar in Taipei on Tuesday.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 23 Dec 2025

  • Security Council remains divided over Iran nuclear programme, sanctions stay in place UN News 23 Dec 2025 -- At a meeting to discuss nuclear non-proliferation on Tuesday, the Security Council's stance on Iran's nuclear activities remained divided on the issue, split between those who support the reinstatement of sanctions, and the members who believe the sanctions should be permanently lifted, whilst questioning the legality of the meeting itself.
  • No Agreement on Way Forward, UN Political Chief Tells Security Council, as Speakers Split over Iran's Nuclear Ambitions, Merits of Snapback Sanctions UN Security Council 23 Dec 2025 -- Iran has provided "no information" to the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) on the status of its enriched uranium stockpiles, a top UN official told the Security Council today, as members split over the "snapback" mechanism and many urged parties to return to negotiations to pursue a diplomatic solution.
  • We call on Iran to urgently lift its restrictions and fully cooperate with the IAEA: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 23 Dec 2025 -- Statement by Ambassador Archie Young, UK Deputy Permanent Representative to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Iran.
  • Iran: France, Britain, US should take practical and credible steps to rebuild trust IRNA 23 Dec 2025 -- Iranian Ambassador and Permanent Representative to the UN Amir Saeid Iravani has strongly criticized the EU troika and the US for their lack of commitment to the JCPOA and breaching the previous deals.
  • Iran has 'inalienable right' to maintain peaceful nuclear program: Russian envoy IRNA 23 Dec 2025 -- Russia's Permanent Representative to international organizations in Vienna, Mikhail Ulyanov, has emphasized that Iran has the right under the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT) to continue its peaceful nuclear program.
  • 'Unrealistic': Russia warns against expanding Iran talks beyond nuclear file Press TV 23 Dec 2025 -- Russia has warned that any attempt to broaden talks with Iran beyond its nuclear program would render negotiations "unrealistic," reinforcing Tehran's insistence that its missile and defense capabilities are not up for discussion.
  • Iran halts gas exports to Iraq amid surge in domestic demand Press TV 23 Dec 2025 -- Iran has temporarily halted its natural gas exports to neighboring Iraq due to a sharp rise in domestic demand for heating.

  • Joint Declaration - The 10th Trilateral Summit of Israel, Greece, and Cyprus Israel - Prime Minister 23 Dec 2025
  • Netanyahu appears in court for 65th time in ongoing corruption trial Press TV 23 Dec 2025 -- Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has appeared before the Tel Aviv District Court for the 65th time to respond to corruption charges in cases that have haunted him throughout much of his premiership, according to Israeli media reports.

  • Poland hands over instrument of ratification of Nancy Treaty Poland - Ministry of Foreign Affairs 23 Dec 2025 -- Undersecretary of State Henryka Mościcka-Dendys presented French Ambassador Étienne de Poncins with the instrument of ratification of the Treaty for Enhanced Cooperation and Friendship between the Republic of Poland and the French Republic.

  • The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Chairs Cabinet Session SPA 23 Dec 2025 -- Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud chaired the Cabinet session held today in Riyadh

  • Parliamentary Vice-Minister for Foreign Affairs ONISHI's Visit to the Syrian Arab Republic Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 23 Dec 2025

  • Bangladesh: UN expert outraged by violent attacks against Daily Star and Prothom Alo OHCHR 23 Dec 2025 -- Mob attacks on leading media outlets and cultural centres in Bangladesh are deeply alarming and must be investigated promptly and effectively by the government, and the perpetrators brought to justice without delay, a UN expert* said today.

  • The UK stands firmly with the people of Venezuela in their pursuit of a democratic future: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 23 Dec 2025 -- Statement by Ambassador Archie Young, UK Deputy Permanent Representative to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Venezuela.

  • World News in Brief: Gaza aid restrictions, Syria humanitarian needs, lawyer reprisals in Burundi UN News 23 Dec 2025 -- The UN and partners continue to provide assistance to people in Gaza, where recent winter storms have worsened conditions for thousands across the enclave.
